stories.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
stories.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.

stories.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
stories.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

stories.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
stories.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

stories.title: Former national security adviser discusses the ongoing talks between the U.S. and Iran
stories.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Biden administration national security adviser Jake Sullivan for his reaction to the latest developments in the war in Iran.

stories.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
stories.description: Gulf states, led by Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, are urging Trump to continue prosecuting the war against Iran, arguing that Tehran hasn't been weakened enough by the bombing campaign, according to U.S., Gulf and Israeli officials. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with ...

stories.title: Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount
stories.description: France and Italy have joined Spain in pushing back on U.S.-Israeli military operations, sources said on Tuesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump singled out NATO allies in Europe as unhelpful in the month-long war, highlighting divisions. The decisions came against a backdrop of t...

stories.title: Can Lebanon keep it together? Government faces Israeli invasion, standoff with Hezbollah
stories.description: The Lebanese have seen it all, from civil war to the 2019 protests demanding an end to revolving door politics, from the 2020 port of Beirut explosion that exposed the tragic consequences of nepotism to Israel’s 2024 operation to neutralize longtime foe Hezbollah. Now Israel’s gr...

stories.title: #Iran: Massive explosion in Isfahan
stories.description: Images posted on social media on March 31 have captured a huge #explosion in the Iranian city of #Isfahan. A local security official said strikes had targeted military locations in the city, according to Iran’s Fars agency. Isfahan is home to a #nuclear enrichment site.

stories.title: Israel to occupy swaths of south Lebanon and destroy houses near border
stories.description: Israel's defence minister has vowed his army will block residents from returning to southern Lebanon after the war and will be destroying all homes in villages along the border, in which he implied would be a Gaza-like scale of destruction as seen in Rafah and Beit Hanoun. More t...

stories.title: King Charles III to make state visit to the US in April despite calls to cancel over Iran war
stories.description: The UK's King Charles III will visit the US and Bermuda in April, Buckingham Palace announced on Tuesday, as critics called for the trip to be cancelled due to concerns over the US's ongoing war with Iran.

stories.title: Iran desalination plant in the Strait of Hormuz out of service following strikes
stories.description: One of the desalination plants on Iran's Qeshm Island has been out ​of ‌service since being ⁠hit by an air strike earlier ‌in the month, an official from ⁠the Health Ministry told Iranian media on March 30. This follows strikes on the cities of Tehran and Isfahan by the US as it ...

stories.title: How Trump’s dream of seizing enriched uranium from Iran could end in catastrophe
stories.description: US President Donald Trump is reportedly considering a military mission to seize Iranian uranium that has been enriched to 60 percent. But while some might view such a plan as offering a quick win, others warn that it could be "one of the most risky and difficult operations the US...

stories.title: How Pakistan positioned itself at the center of global crisis management
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a global crisis mediator amid the US war on Iran.

stories.title: What’s behind Donald Trump’s interest in Iran’s Kharg Island?
stories.description: The US president is threatening to take over Iran's oil export hub or destroy it.

stories.title: Analysis: Hezbollah backs Iran ambassador as conflict deepens Lebanon rifts
stories.description: Lebanon’s expulsion of Iranian ambassador tests limits of state power as Hezbollah fights push for disarmament.

stories.title: Iran’s FM confirms contact with US envoy Witkoff, denies talks under way
stories.description: Iran's top diplomat Araghchi has told Al Jazeera that, despite contacts, Iran is not in negotiations with the US.

stories.title: US-Israel launch major attacks as Iranian authorities maintain defiance
stories.description: Bunker-buster bombs trigger massive secondary explosions at a mountainous area near Isfahan overnight.

stories.title: One month of war on Iran cost Arab countries up to $194bn: UNDP
stories.description: Even if conflict is 'short but intense', countries like Sudan, Yemen, Lebanon will see major increases in poverty rates.

stories.title: Why Iran believes it can outlast the US
stories.description: Iran is preparing for deeper US escalation while insisting it’s winning. Can this war end before it widens?

stories.title: Qatar says ‘ready to help’ with US-Iran negotiations
stories.description: Qatari Foreign Ministry spokesperson Majed al-Ansari says Qatar is “ready to help” with US-Iran negotiations.

stories.title: Trump attacks NATO allies as pressure mounts over Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Alan Fisher explains what Trump’s latest criticism of US allies means.

stories.title: Qatar says Iran’s attacks on neighbours crossed ‘many red lines’
stories.description: Iranian attacks on Qatar are having a 'catastrophic effect on the relationship between the two countries', Doha says.

stories.title: Iran will play World Cup games in US despite war, FIFA’s Infantino says
stories.description: The chief of global football's governing body says US-Israeli war on Iran will not affect scheduling of 2026 World Cup.

stories.title: Where to invest during Iran war? Ask – or don’t – Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf
stories.description: Iran’s parliamentary speaker is using X to shape market sentiment, blending war messaging with financial signalling.

stories.title: King Charles to pay state visit to US as UK seeks to mollify Trump
stories.description: King Charles will make a state visit to the United States in late April, Buckingham Palace said on Tuesday, a high-profile trip that the British government hopes will help to repair relations with President Donald Trump, damaged by the war on Iran. Charles and his wife Queen Cami...

stories.title: Pakistan, China issue 5-point initiative for restoring peace in Middle East
stories.description: Pakistan and China on Tuesday issued a five-point initiative for restoring peace and stability in the Gulf and the Middle East amid the US-Israeli war on Iran, in which they called on the immediate cessation of hostilities and starting peace talks as soon as possible. The develop...

stories.title: Pakistan, seven other countries condemn Israeli curbs on Muslim and Christian worship in occupied Jerusalem
stories.description: Pakistan, along with seven other countries, condemned the restrictions imposed by Israel on the freedom of worship for Muslims and Christians in occupied Jerusalem, the Foreign Office (FO) said on Tuesday. Since Israel and the United States started a war with Iran on Feb 28, Isra...

stories.title: ‘Diplomacy takes time’: Pak envoy reaffirms Islamabad’s commitment to Iran-US peace amid complex situation
stories.description: Pakistan’s Ambassador to the US Rizwan Saeed Sheikh has reaffirmed Islamabad’s commitment to facilitating dialogue between the United States and Iran, even as regional tensions intensify. In an interview broadcast on Tuesday, Ambassador Sheikh told Fox News that Pakistan is “comm...

stories.title: US sends third aircraft carrier strike group to Middle East — newspaper
stories.description: The redeployment is taking place as US President Donald Trump is mulling further military options against Iran

stories.title: Hezbollah launches over 50 projectiles, drones at northern Israel
stories.description: According to the movement, the targets were attacked with "a barrage of advanced missiles and a swarm of attack drones"

stories.title: IRGC says about 200 US troops killed, wounded in attack on airbase in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: According to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, the majority of those killed and wounded are fighter pilots

stories.title: US not even considering operation to seize Iran’s nuclear materials — Trump
stories.description: Previously, the US government had not ruled out attempting to seize these materials

stories.title: Iranian Interior Minister Momeni thanks Russia, other allies for humanitarian aid
stories.description: Eskandar Momeni called these actions a manifestation of solidarity and cooperation between friendly countries

stories.title: Iran seeks complete end to Middle East war rather than ceasefire — top diplomat
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi added that Iran’s conditions include guarantees that aggression will not recur

stories.title: Attacks damage WHO office in Tehran, leaving no casualties — WHO chief
stories.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus noted that such attacks "cannot be tolerated and must be avoided at all costs"

stories.title: US Secretary of State asks G7 to participate in international mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: According to the source, Marco Rubio still indicated that the US does not need G7 assistance in opening the strait

stories.title: Iranian filmmaker Majidi suggests that MFA demand US extradite Pahlavi
stories.description: Majid Majidi is considered one of the most authoritative and well-known filmmakers in Iran

stories.title: UK announces shortlist for International Booker Prize
stories.description: The books are Shida Bazar’s 'The Nights Are Quiet in Tehran' and Daniel Kehlmann’s 'The Director'

stories.title: US, Israeli strikes damage over 39,500 civilian facilities in Tehran, says official
stories.description: Fatemeh Mohajerani said that 105,325 civilian sites were attacked, including 83,351 residential buildings and 21,059 commercial properties

stories.title: Israel, US carry out strikes on 24 pharmaceutical industry facilities — Iranian minister
stories.description: The official said that medical equipment production and supply chains have been seriously damaged

stories.title: Russian citizens wishing to return home via Azerbaijan leave Iran — envoy
stories.description: Alexey Dedov noted that foreign nationals need a special code to cross Azerbaijan’s border

stories.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten to attack US tech firms in Mideast
stories.description: According to the statement, the US companies in the sphere of information technology and artificial intelligence are a key element in working out and tracking targets for the attacks

stories.title: Russia ready to facilitate resolution of Middle East conflict — ambassador to Iran
stories.description: Alexey Dedov said that Moscow advocates an immediate cessation of hostilities by the parties to the conflict and the adoption of comprehensive measures to ensure the safety of civilians and civilian infrastructure in all Middle Eastern countries

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
